findings.title: httpx.AsyncClient created at module scope with no lifecycle management — never closed
findings.detail: _client = httpx.AsyncClient(timeout=5.0) is instantiated at import time and never closed. The FastAPI lifespan handler in main.py does not call _client.aclose(). This leaks the connection pool on every server restart (including uvicorn --reload reloads). httpx emits ResourceWarning in some runtimes. On shutdown the event loop may close before the client, producing Task was destroyed but it is pending noise that masks real errors in logs.
findings.recommendation: Register _client.aclose() in the FastAPI lifespan finally block. Expose async def close_client(): await _client.aclose() from notifications.py and call it alongside manager.shutdown() in main.py.

findings.title: asyncio.ensure_future() called from a sync IBKR callback — event loop context not guaranteed
findings.detail: _on_ibkr_error() is a plain synchronous method registered as an IBKR error listener. asyncio.ensure_future(notify_error(...)) is called inside it. ib_insync dispatches error callbacks on its EReader thread, not on the asyncio event loop thread. ensure_future() raises RuntimeError: no running event loop when called from a thread with no current loop, which is an unhandled exception in the callback thread and silently drops the notification.
findings.recommendation: Capture the loop at bot start (self._loop = asyncio.get_event_loop() in run()). Replace asyncio.ensure_future(notify_error(...)) with self._loop.call_soon_threadsafe(asyncio.ensure_future, notify_error(...)). Alternatively, defer the notification to _tick() by inspecting state.status == error at tick entry.

findings.title: notify_exit and notify_stop awaited sequentially inside _tick() — up to 10 s of blocking
findings.detail: On a detected external exit where exit_reason is stop_loss or trailing_stop, _tick() awaits notify_exit() then notify_stop() back-to-back. Each httpx POST has a 5 s timeout. Worst case (ntfy.sh slow, both time out): _tick() blocks 10 s. The poll interval for 1m bars is 10 s, meaning the next bar is missed entirely. The same applies to notify_entry() at line 391. This contradicts the fire-and-forget intent.
findings.recommendation: Replace bare await with asyncio.create_task(): asyncio.create_task(notify_exit(...)) and asyncio.create_task(notify_entry(...)). The 5 s timeout in notify() still bounds per-request hang time, but _tick() returns immediately regardless of ntfy.sh latency.

findings.title: NOTIFY_URL cached forever on first read — malformed URL silently retried on every notification
findings.detail: _notify_url_checked prevents re-reading the environment after the first call. If NOTIFY_URL is malformed (missing scheme, trailing whitespace, typo), the bad value is cached and passed to _client.post() on every notification attempt. Each call raises and is swallowed by the except block, logged as a warning — correct behavior — but the operator has no startup-time signal that the URL is bad.
findings.recommendation: Validate the URL at cache time with httpx.URL(url) inside try/except and log a clear warning immediately if it is malformed, so the misconfiguration is visible at startup rather than buried in per-notification warning logs.

findings.title: Test endpoint catch block is dead code — notify() never raises, so sent=False is never returned
findings.detail: send_test_notification() wraps await notify() in try/except and returns {sent: False, reason: ...} on exception. But notify() has its own blanket except that swallows all errors and never propagates. The outer catch never fires. A user with a misconfigured NOTIFY_URL sees {sent: True} even though the notification was silently dropped, making the test endpoint misleading.
findings.recommendation: Expose a notify_with_result() variant that propagates exceptions (no inner except), and use it in the test endpoint. Or have notify() return bool success and propagate that through.

residual_risks.title: ntfy.sh outage causes tick delays during stop cascade
residual_risks.detail: With R3 unaddressed, back-to-back notify_exit + notify_stop timeouts (10 s) on a stop_loss exit delay the next tick. With create_task (R3 fix) this risk disappears.

residual_risks.title: Single shared httpx client pool across all bots and the route layer
residual_risks.detail: All concurrent bot ticks and the test endpoint share one httpx.AsyncClient. Under normal bot counts and ntfy.sh volumes this is fine; httpx default pool limits (100 connections) make exhaustion unlikely.

testing_gaps.title: No test for notify() with a malformed NOTIFY_URL
testing_gaps.detail: A test setting NOTIFY_URL to not-a-url should verify a warning is logged and no exception propagates. Currently untested.

testing_gaps.title: No test for _on_ibkr_error() ensure_future behavior from a non-loop thread
testing_gaps.detail: The ensure_future() call in _on_ibkr_error has no test simulating a non-asyncio-thread callback, so the RuntimeError failure mode in R2 cannot be caught by CI.
